preview.summary: A Group D meeting with knockout-qualification stakes as Turkey, back at a World Cup for the first time since their 2002 run, face co-hosts USA in front of a partisan LA crowd. Turkey carry one of the tournament's most exciting young cores, while the USMNT lean on a settled spine and home-field momentum. With both sides eyeing the round of 32, the result could shape the entire group.
preview.homeForm: Reached the finals via the European playoffs in March, their first appearance since 2002.
preview.awayForm: As co-hosts with no qualifying, Pochettino's side enters with a long runway and a settled core.
preview.homeKey: Arda Guler, Real Madrid playmaker and Turkey's creative spark
preview.awayKey: Christian Pulisic, the most-capped USMNT player and talisman
storylines: Turkey's first World Cup since their 2002 third-place finish
USMNT's home-soil push to reach the knockouts
Guler and Yildiz vs a Pulisic-led American attack
preview.h2h: Tight history across four meetings, with the USA holding a narrow edge but Turkey winning the most recent, a 2-1 friendly in June 2025.
